segments.text: The word of the Lord came to me again, saying,
Son of man, say to the prince of Tyre,
Because your heart is lifted up, And you say, 'I am a god, I sit in the seat of gods, In the midst of the seas,' Yet you are a man, and not a god, Though you set your heart as the heart of a god
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: Behold, you are wiser than Daniel! There is no secret that can be hidden from you!
With your wisdom and your understanding You have gained riches for yourself, And gathered gold and silver into your treasuries;
By your great wisdom in trade you have increased your riches, And your heart is lifted up because of your riches
Because you have set your heart as the heart of a god,
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: Behold, therefore, I will bring strangers against you, The most terrible of the nations; And they shall draw their swords against the beauty of your wisdom, And defile your splendor.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: They shall throw you down into the Pit, And you shall die the death of the slain In the midst of the seas.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: Will you still say before him who slays you, 'I am a god'? But you shall be a man, and not a god, In the hand of him who slays you.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: You shall die the death of the uncircumcised By the hand of aliens; For I have spoken,
segments.speaker: The Lord God

verses.title: Lamentation for the King of Tyre

segments.text: Moreover the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
Son of man, take up a lamentation for the king of Tyre, and say to him,
You were the seal of perfection, Full of wisdom and perfect in beauty.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: You were in Eden, the garden of God; Every precious stone was your covering: The sardius, topaz, and diamond, Beryl, onyx, and jasper, Sapphire, turquoise, and emerald with gold. The workmanship of your timbrels and pipes Was prepared for you on the day you were created.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: You were the anointed cherub who covers; I established you; You were on the holy mountain of God; You walked back and forth in the midst of fiery stones.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: You were perfect in your ways from the day you were created, Till iniquity was found in you.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: By the abundance of your trading You became filled with violence within, And you sinned; Therefore I cast you as a profane thing Out of the mountain of God; And I destroyed you, O covering cherub, From the midst of the fiery stones.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: Your heart was lifted up because of your beauty; You corrupted your wisdom for the sake of your splendor; I cast you to the ground, I laid you before kings, That they might gaze at you.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: You defiled your sanctuaries By the multitude of your iniquities, By the iniquity of your trading; Therefore I brought fire from your midst; It devoured you, And I turned you to ashes upon the earth In the sight of all who saw you.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: All who knew you among the peoples are astonished at you; You have become a horror, And shall be no more forever.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: Then the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
Son of man, set your face toward Sidon, and prophesy against her,
and say,
Behold, I am against you, O Sidon; I will be glorified in your midst; And they shall know that I am the Lord, When I execute judgments in her and am hallowed in her.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: For I will send pestilence upon her, And blood in her streets; The wounded shall be judged in her midst By the sword against her on every side; Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: And there shall no longer be a pricking brier or a painful thorn for the house of Israel from among all who are around them, who despise them. Then they shall know that I am the Lord God.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: When I have gathered the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they are scattered, and am hallowed in them in the sight of the Gentiles, then they will dwell in their own land which I gave to My servant Jacob.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: And they will dwell safely there, build houses, and plant vineyards; yes, they will dwell securely, when I execute judgments on all those around them who despise them. Then they shall know that I am the Lord their God.
segments.speaker: The Lord God
